title: mentha × piperita var. hircina
intro: Mentha × piperita var. hircina, also known as wild bergamot mint, is a variety of mint that is native to North America and is characterized by its distinctive scented leaves and purple flowers. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, making it effective in treating digestive issues, sore throats, and skin irritations. The health benefits of wild bergamot mint include its ability to reduce stress, improve digestion, and boost the immune system, as well as its potential to alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Wild bergamot mint can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and topical creams, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and enjoy its numerous health benefits.
